cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which answer choice is most similar to Georgia O'Keeffe's relationship with nature. Let me start by reading the passage again to understand her relationship better. The passage says that O'Keeffe was born in Wisconsin, grew up on a farm, and never lost her bond with the land. She painted the things that were most important to her, which are simplified portrayals of nature. She painted desert landscapes, cows' skulls, bones, rocks, hills, and flowers. After her husband died, she moved to New Mexico permanently and continued painting the desert until her death. She's considered a modernist painter and her work is more identifiably American, focusing on simplified and idealized treatments of natural forms.
